title: Gnyabagam Varugiradha Lyrics Vishwaroopam 2
stanzas:
Gnyabagam Varugiradha - ivan
thee endru therigiratha
thadaigalai vendre
sarithiram padaithavan
Gnyabagam Varugiradha

neerindri verillia
ivan yaarukkum arasanillai
kaadugal thaandi
kadakindra pozhudhum
kaatrukku kaayamillai.

yevan endru ninaithaai
edhai kandu sirithai
velippadum
pudhu suyaroobam

neruppuku pirandhaan
nitham valarndhaan
velippadum
pudhu suyaroobam

vishwaroopam
roopam roopam (3)

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song Gnyabagam Varugiradha?

The song explores themes of memory and longing, reflecting on past experiences and emotions.

How does Gnyabagam Varugiradha fit into the plot of Vishwaroopam 2?

This song encapsulates the protagonist's emotional journey, highlighting moments of reflection and sorrow.

Who are the singers of the song?

The song features the talented voices of Arvind Srinivas and Sarath Santhosh.

Who wrote the lyrics for Gnyabagam Varugiradha?

The lyrics were penned by the renowned lyricist Vairamuthu.

When was Gnyabagam Varugiradha released?

The song was released on July 31, 2018.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Gnyabagam Varugiradha, the listeners are taken on an emotional journey through the lens of nostalgia and longing. The song beautifully captures the essence of reflecting on past relationships and the bittersweet memories associated with them. With its soothing melody and heartfelt lyrics, it evokes a sense of melancholy that resonates deeply. This duet serves as a poignant reminder of the complexities of love and loss, making it a standout piece in Vishwaroopam 2.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Gnyabagam Varugiradha from Vishwaroopam 2 is a hauntingly beautiful duet that captures the essence of longing and introspection. With the soulful voices of Arvind Srinivas and Sarath Santhosh, this song resonates deeply with anyone who has experienced heartache. The poignant lyrics by Vairamuthu elevate the emotional depth, making it a must-listen for fans of Tamil music.
stanzaMeanings: The memory is coming back - he is the one who created history by saying he is fire.
Without water, he is not a king to anyone; even when crossing forests, the wind has no permanence.
Who do you think he is? Seeing this, a new form of laughter emerges.
Born from fire, he grows every day, revealing a new form, the universal form, form, form.
The memory is coming back - he is the one who created history by saying he is fire.
Without water, he is not a king to anyone; even when crossing forests, the wind has no permanence.
Who do you think he is? Seeing this, a new form of laughter emerges.
